bio1: I'm a graphic designer with 5+ years of experience across poster design, social media content, pitch deck, brand identity, digital art, and event branding. I've spent that time freelancing on Fiverr, working with clients who needed their brand to look sharper and get noticed faster.
bio2: My approach is simple: every piece should have a clear focal point, confident typography, and a mood that fits exactly where it's going to live, whether that's a phone feed, a boardroom screen, or a cinema wall.
bio3: I'm taking on new freelance projects directly. If you need identity, posters, campaign creatives, or a deck that makes people lean in, let's talk.

projects.title: Epilytix Brand Identity
projects.subtitle: Premium Brand Ecosystem & Visual Strategy
projects.details:
The Mission:
As the co-founder and lead designer of Epilytix, I needed to build a brand identity that felt tech-forward but distinctly human. The goal was to break away from standard, cold corporate tech aesthetics and craft a vibrant, premium ecosystem that signals innovation, fluid problem-solving, and authority in the software space.

The Execution:
The identity centers on the "epx" monogram, using continuous, fluid lines to represent integration and seamless data flow. I curated a bold, high-contrast color palette, blending electric magenta and deep tech-purples against stark monochrome tones, to ensure high visual impact across digital interfaces and physical apparel. By stress-testing the mark across diverse mockups, from minimal stationery to clean mobile app icons and embroidered merchandise, I created an adaptable design system that positions Epilytix as an agile, cutting-edge software powerhouse.

projects.title: Mind in Chaos
projects.subtitle: Cinematic Horror Key Art & Theatrical Poster Design
projects.details:
The Mission:
Horror key art thrives on psychological tension, so my goal was to visually map the concept of a fractured sanity. I wanted to design a premium, industry-standard theatrical poster that establishes a chilling narrative contrast between the physical world and a looming, predatory subconscious nightmare.

The Execution:
I engineered a classic, top-heavy vertical hierarchy, positioning a hyper-detailed, Nosferatu-inspired creature as the dominant psychological shadow looming over the characters. To visually isolate the subjects from the cold, blue-toned background environment, I flooded the midground mansion and characters with an ominous, low-angle crimson red glow. The design is anchored by a heavily textured, distressed crimson typography treatment that bleeds into the foreground reflections, giving the entire composition a visceral, high-end indie-horror aesthetic.

projects.title: The Horizon Hunter
projects.subtitle: Cinematic Product Key Art & Conceptual Automotive Poster
projects.details:
The Mission:
My goal was to break away from static, traditional car shots and transform the Nissan GT-R into a theatrical experience. I wanted to tell a story of raw speed and legacy by engineering a dynamic, multi-tiered visual narrative.

The Execution:
I stacked the vehicle's best design elements into a high-impact vertical composition, blending its aggressive stance with a macro close-up of its bodywork. To give it an untamed, aggressive edge, I utilized a hyper-saturated, molten orange palette wrapped in heavy environmental smoke and golden backlighting. Grounding the car on a dark, wet asphalt texture allowed me to build high-contrast reflections, creating a premium, heavy, and cinematic feel.

projects.title: Isabeau Key Art
projects.subtitle: Dark Fantasy by Butterfly Entertainment
projects.details:
The Mission:
With this poster, I wanted to capture the sweeping, epic scale of a high-end dark fantasy narrative. My goal was to create a dense, character-driven key art design that immediately builds an atmospheric world full of mystery, magic, and impending conflict, making it feel like promotional material for a premium streaming series or film.

The Execution:
I designed a layered triangular composition, anchoring the poster with a prominent, piercing-eyed protagonist in the foreground to create an immediate emotional connection. To build out the narrative depth, I balanced the midground with ominous, symbolic wildlife—a golden-eyed wolf and a red-eyed steed—while stacking the background with hooded figures and a soaring raven. I utilized a desaturated, misty forest color palette heavily leaning into cold blues and deep grays, which makes the subtle eye glows and the intricate, chiseled stone typography pop with a timeless, high-fantasy aesthetic.
